{{Quote|There is a place in the galaxy where the dark side of the Force runs strong. It is something of the Sith, but it was fueled by war. It corrupts all that walks on its surface—drowns them in the power of the dark side. It corrupts all life, and it feeds on death.|[[Kreia]]|audio=KreiaMalachorV.flac|<ref name="KotOR II" />}}
'''Malachor V''', or simply '''Malachor''', was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Malachor system/Legends|Malachor system]]. Prior to the [[Battle of Malachor V|final battle]] of the [[Mandalorian Wars]], the planet was fertile and hospitable. After the cataclysm caused by the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]], it became a cracked and twisted wasteland covered with jagged cliffs and plagued by constant lightning storms. It was inhabited by monstrous [[storm beast]]s. The [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] of the planet was unstable, which made it dangerous for [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to stay there for very long.

==History==
===Early history===
{{Quote|And because Malachor, like Korriban, is on the fringes of the ancient Sith Empire, where the Sith wait for us, in the dark.|Kreia|<ref name="KotOR II" />}}
[[File:MalachorVIntact.jpg|thumb|left|Malachor V before the Cataclysm.]]

At some point after [[27,700 BBY]] but before the fall of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]], the [[Sith Empire (Pre-Republic)|Sith Empire]] colonized Malachor V.<ref name="TEA" />

"Thousands of years" before the [[Mandalorian Wars]], a Sith Empire built the [[Trayus Academy]] on Malachor V.<ref name="KotOR II" /> 

At some point, [[Dakar Sol]] led a [[Malachor V rebellion|rebellion]] against the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] on Malachor V.<ref name="Codex" />

By the time of the [[Mandalorian Wars]], Malachor V was abandoned.<ref name="KotOR II" />

===Mandalorian Wars===
{{Quote|It was nothing more than a slaughter. A slaughter caused by one of my creations. Blame lies with me, for creating it. The situation forced your hand, anger forced mine. You realized that unless action was taken, the fleet would be destroyed, and the Republic would fall. None of us realized the magnitude of what we unleashed.|[[Bao-Dur]], to [[Meetra Surik]]|<ref name="KotOR II" />}}
[[File:Malchorlandscape.jpg|thumb|left|The barren, harsh landscape of Malachor V]]

Malachor was the site of the final battle of the [[Mandalorian Wars]], during which the worst atrocity of the war was committed. Malachor was restricted to Mandalorians, a taboo in [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] culture, something that the [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] used against them in order to win the war. Revan amassed an enormous fleet at the planet, providing the Mandalorians with a target that was too good to pass up. At the head of this force Revan placed one of his top generals: [[Meetra Surik]]<ref name="Revan" />, who was later known as the ''Jedi Exile''.<ref name="KotOR II" />

During the [[Battle of Malachor V]], Revan single-handedly slew [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] aboard the warlord's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]].<ref name="Revan" /> As Republic and Mandalorian forces outsystem from the planet itself continued to clash, Surik ordered the activation of the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]] superweapon with a single, wordless nod towards its inventor, [[Bao-Dur]], in order to prevent the Mandalorian forces they faced from overwhelming them and moving on to attack Revan's forces from behind. In an instant, Malachor's gravity crushed every combatant in and around its atmosphere—Mandalorian, Republic, and Jedi alike. Malachor's surface was transformed into a barren, shattered and lifeless wasteland—all of Revan's enemies were eliminated in a single stroke. The planet was left scarred from the battle as the surface had been obliterated. Long afterward, there was still evidence of crashed Republic cruisers buried deep within the surface.<ref name="KotOR II" />

===Jedi Civil War===
Following his victory, Revan journeyed into the Unknown Regions with Malak in search of Mandalore's mysterious masters, which the pair learned to be Sith on the ice world of [[Rekkiad]]. However, the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] ultimately turned them to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and sent them back to the Republic to wage war. But the Emperor underestimated the pair's resolve, as they broke free of his commands not long after and decided to conquer the Republic for themselves and adopted the title of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]].<ref name="Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref> During the [[Jedi Civil War]], circa [[3959 BBY|3959]]–[[3956 BBY]], he used the Trayus Academy, located on the planet's surface as one of several secret Sith bases dedicated to the conversion of captured [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. Throughout the war, Jedi were captured by his Sith Assassins and other specially trained forces, and brought to the Academy. The corruptive nature of the location weakened their wills so that Revan and his loyal servants could easily lure them to the dark side. In such places, he converted hosts of Jedi into fanatical [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]] for his [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]].<ref name="KotOR II" />

Several years after the war had ended, Revan's old Master, [[Kreia]], traveled to Malachor V in search of what had corrupted her apprentice. Like Revan, she was corrupted by the dark side energy resonating throughout the planet; in secret, she then took the moniker of Darth Traya and began training new students of the dark side at the Trayus Academy, which had remained unscathed despite the devastation wrought by the Mass Shadow Generator. Among her two students were [[Darth Sion]] and [[Darth Nihilus]]. The three Sith Lords formed an [[Sith Triumvirate|uneasy alliance]] against the Jedi, but the two apprentices eventually became powerful enough to overthrow Traya. Sion and Nihilus broke the alliance and cast Traya down, stripped her of her power, and exiled her from Malachor.<ref name="KotOR II" />

===Final destruction===
{{GameLS}}
In [[3951 BBY]], Kreia located Meetra Surik, the only Jedi who had fought at Malachor V and not been killed or corrupted. Plotting revenge against her apprentices, Kreia began orchestrating events to set up a final confrontation between herself and Surik at the Trayus Academy. After [[Battle of Telos IV|slaying Darth Nihilus]], Surik arrived at the Academy and [[Battle of Malachor|killed the Sith there]], including Darth Sion and Darth Traya.<ref name="KotOR II" />

While Surik fought in the Academy, Bao-Dur ordered his remote to reactivate the Mass Shadow Generator in an effort to destroy the tainted world of Malachor V once and for all. Despite [[G0-T0]]'s attempts to thwart it, the remote succeeded in its task. As Surik departed Malachor in the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', the dead planet crumbled under the pressure of the [[Mass shadow/Legends|mass shadows']] gravity, leaving behind only asteroids and the wreckage of many hundreds of blasted warships, a grim memorial to the Battle of Malachor V, and thus silencing the echoes of the atrocity that, until then, Malachor had still sent through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="KotOR II" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Records say that the Republic installed the Mass Shadow Generator at Malachor V near the end of the Mandalorian Wars. Did it work? There's no Malachor V anymore, so it certainly did ''something''.|Bevel Lemelisk, ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''|<ref name="Imperial Handbook">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>}}
By [[3640 BBY]],<ref name="Encyclopedia">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> surveys showed small pockets of biological life were still active in Malachor V's remains.<ref name="Legacy of Malachor">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Bioanalysis|Legacy of Malachor}}</ref> It was also the site of an excavation for remaining artifacts and treasures.<ref name="DigIt">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Treasure Hunting|Dig It}}</ref>

The planet's infamy led to its name becoming a curse word, synonymous with "[[Chaos/Legends|Hell]]." By the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Republic [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Meebur Gascon/Legends|Meebur Gascon]] used it in anger.<ref name="MIA">{{TCW|Missing in Action}}</ref>

The planet, alongside its infamous use of the mass shadow generator and its destruction, was later used as an example of ancient superweapons by [[Bevel Lemelisk]] of the [[Imperial Department of Military Research/Legends|Imperial Department of Military Research]] in the ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'', the official field manual for the Imperial Military. Upon finding this reference to the weapon and Malachor V, Luke Skywalker pondered whether he should investigate the planet for possible artifacts belonging to either the Jedi or the Sith.<ref name="Imperial Handbook" />

==Behind the scenes==
If the player chooses to take the path of the dark side in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', Meetra Surik throws Darth Traya's body into the abyss and takes the title of Dark Lord of the Sith while the planet survives.<ref name="KotOR II" />
